_doc: Analytical-lab reference data for MOLGANG. samples: realistic bulk assays (element wt%, oxides converted to element basis). lines: strongest ICP-OES emission lines (nm) + handheld-XRF detectability. Verified by an adversarial chemistry pass (see Admin/claude/fable).

samples.name: LD/BOF steel slag (converter slag)
samples.description: Basic-oxygen-furnace / Linz-Donawitz converter slag, the primary feedstock for Ca and V valorisation. Element basis converted from oxide assay (CaO ~44%, Fe-oxides ~22%, SiO2 ~12%, MgO ~7%, MnO ~4%, V2O5 ~1.4%). High basicity (CaO/SiO2 ~3.5), contains free lime.
samples.note: Handheld XRF cannot quantify O or distinguish free-CaO from Ca-silicate/Ca-ferrite speciation, which governs Ca leachability; V ~0.8% sits near the gun's LOD in a heavy Fe/Ca matrix, so confirm the V-recovery grade by fusion + ICP-OES.

samples.name: Stainless steel 316L (RVS316L)
samples.description: Austenitic 316L (EN 1.4404) low-carbon Mo-bearing stainless. Fe balance with 16-18% Cr and (excluded here) Ni/Mo. Representative of tanks/piping in the leaching circuit; also a slag contaminant source.
samples.note: Sum corrected from 100.005 to 99.995 by trimming 'other' 14.80->14.79. The ~14.8% 'other' balance is Ni (~12.5%) + Mo (~2.3%), the diagnostic alloying elements for 316L but outside the allowed symbol set here; XRF sees them fine, but the 0.02% C (the L / low-carbon spec) is below XRF LOD and needs combustion (LECO) to confirm 316L vs 316.

samples.name: Ferrovanadium FeV80
samples.description: Ferrovanadium master alloy, ~80% V, the target refined product of the vanadium-recovery chain. Fe balance with minor Al/Si from the aluminothermic/silicothermic reduction.
samples.note: At 80% V the XRF matrix/inter-element correction is severe and calibration drifts against low-V slag standards; C, P and S (the penalty elements for FeV grade) need combustion/ICP, so treat XRF here as sorting-only and certify by fusion-ICP-OES.

samples.name: Leaded free-cutting brass (CuZn39Pb3)
samples.description: Machining brass, ~59% Cu / ~38% Zn with ~3% Pb added for chip-breaking. Common valve/fitting scrap and a Zn/Cu reference for the gun.
samples.note: 'other' is mainly Sn plus trace Ni not represented here; Pb is present as discrete insoluble globules so a small XRF spot over-/under-reads it depending on where it lands, and a dezincified surface layer biases the Zn:Cu ratio unless you abrade before shooting.

samples.name: Granite (silicate ore reference)
samples.description: Felsic granite, ~72% SiO2 with feldspar-hosted Al, K, Na. Serves as a high-silica geological matrix / grinding-media and gangue reference against the basic slag.
samples.note: Na (and to a lesser extent Mg/Al) have very poor fluorescence yield, so a handheld XRF systematically under-reports the feldspar alkali content; for reliable majors run a fused-bead WD-XRF or ICP-OES after lithium-borate fusion.

samples.name: Gibbsitic bauxite
samples.description: Aluminium ore, ~50% Al2O3 with ~18% Fe2O3 and ~25% loss-on-ignition as structural water (gibbsite). Included as an Al/Fe/Ti oxide ore end-member and an alternative Al source.
samples.note: The ~25 wt% loss-on-ignition (bound water, seen here as H + associated O) is invisible to XRF, so an oxide sum on the gun will not close and Al is over-reported on an as-received basis; dry/ignite and re-run, or use fusion-ICP, and note V/Cr ride along with the Fe phase.

samples.name: High-calcium limestone (CaCO3)
samples.description: ~95% pure calcite limestone, the flux/neutralisation and CaCO3 precipitation reference for the Ca-recovery product. Element basis from CaCO3 stoichiometry plus minor silicate/dolomite impurity.
samples.note: XRF is blind to the C and O that make up ~59% of the mass, so purity is reported as CaCO3-by-difference / LOI rather than measured directly; the small Mg reading is the key handle on dolomite content, which matters for distinguishing recovered calcite from raw limestone.

samples.name: Hot-dip galvanising bottom dross (Zn-Fe, Pb-bearing)
samples.description: Skimmed bottom dross from a hot-dip galvanising bath: Zn-rich with a Zn-Fe intermetallic (hard zinc) plus Pb and Al bath additives. The Zn/Pb end-member of the sample suite.
samples.note: 'other' hides Cd and Sn, which are RoHS/recycling-relevant but not representable here; the Fe is locked in a Zn-Fe intermetallic (heterogeneous, so grind and homogenise before shooting), and any oxidised skimming carries O the gun won't report, biasing the Zn total high.

H.note: Balmer H-alpha 656.279 strongest; H-beta 486.133; Z=1, not a routine ICP-OES analyte

He.note: He I D3 587.562; 388.865; noble gas, Z=2, not quantified by ICP-OES

Li.note: Li I 670.784 resonance doublet very strong; 610.362; Z=3

B.note: B I close doublet 249.678/249.773; 208.959 alternate; Z=5

C.note: C I 193.091 (vacuum-UV, purge needed); 247.856 alternate; Z=6

N.note: vacuum-UV N I 174.272/149.262; not routine (Ar plasma/entrainment interference); Z=7

O.note: O I 777.194 triplet; 130.217 vacuum-UV; not a routine analyte; Z=8

F.note: F I 685.603 weak, high excitation; 690.246; poor sensitivity; Z=9

Ne.note: Ne I 585.249/640.225; noble gas, Z=10, not quantified by ICP-OES

Na.note: Na D resonance doublet 588.995/589.592; XRF marginal (low-Z, Z=11)

Mg.note: Mg II 279.553 strongest; Mg I 285.213; Z=12 boundary case, handheld XRF quantification marginal/unreliable so treated as undetectable

Al.note: Al I 396.152; Al II 167.079 vacuum-UV most sensitive; Z=13

S.note: S I vacuum-UV lines; 180.731 primary, purge required; 182.034

Cl.note: Cl I 134.724 vacuum-UV; 725.67; poor ICP-OES sensitivity

K.note: K I resonance doublet in far-red 766.49/769.896

Ca.note: Ca II H&K 393.366/396.847 very strong; Ca I 422.673 alt

Zn.note: Zn I 213.856 primary; Zn II 206.200 alt; 202.548

Br.note: Br I vacuum-UV 154.065/148.845; poor ICP-OES sensitivity

Pb.note: Pb II 220.353 primary; Pb I 217.000/283.306 alt

Ar.note: plasma support gas, not a solid-sample analyte
